title: Occupational exposure respiratory documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.
assessment.rationale: Supports occupational-asthma history and objective-test context for occupational respiratory exposure documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or diagnosed asthma, excluding severe asthma and acute attacks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist diagnosis, monitoring, and chronic asthma management.
source.exclusions: Exact for chronic asthma assessment and monitoring; excludes severe asthma and acute attacks and is not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

assessment.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
assessment.rationale: Supports occupational and environmental exposure review in chronic cough for occupational respiratory exposure documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
source.population: Adults with chronic cough lasting more than 8 weeks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment of chronic cough.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic cough assessment and referral; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

assessment.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.
assessment.rationale: Supports trigger, symptom-pattern and occupational-context review in rhinitis for occupational respiratory exposure documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
source.setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
source.exclusions: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_location.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.
